courseName: (Free Solution) NURS FPX4900 Odom Jonell Assessment2 1

questions.question: A patient with (Free Solution) NURS FPX4900 Odom Jonell Assessment2 presents with sudden shortness of breath, tachycardia, and anxiety. What is the nurse's highest priority action?
options: Assess airway patency and respiratory effort
Document the symptoms in the chart
questions.explanation: Airway and breathing are the highest priorities in an acute respiratory emergency before medication or diagnostic tests.

questions.question: Which finding is most consistent with early (Free Solution) NURS FPX4900 Odom Jonell Assessment2 decompensation in a hospitalized patient?
options: Stable blood pressure with warm skin
questions.explanation: In early decompensation, respiratory distress and mental status changes appear before stable vital signs improve.

questions.question: A nurse is teaching a patient about managing (Free Solution) NURS FPX4900 Odom Jonell Assessment2. Which statement shows the patient understands the plan?
options: I will skip my medication when I feel better
I will only use oxygen when the nurse is present
I will stop water intake completely
I will call the clinic if my breathing gets worse
questions.explanation: Appropriate self-monitoring and knowing when to seek help are key patient education outcomes.

questions.question: The best nursing diagnosis for a patient experiencing (Free Solution) NURS FPX4900 Odom Jonell Assessment2 with fluid overload is:
options: Excess fluid volume related to impaired cardiac output
Risk for infection related to intravenous therapy
Impaired skin integrity related to immobility
questions.explanation: Fluid overload linked to cardiac or renal compromise is best described as excess fluid volume.

questions.question: Which lab value should the nurse monitor most closely for a patient with (Free Solution) NURS FPX4900 Odom Jonell Assessment2?
questions.explanation: Potassium fluctuations are especially important in patients with cardiopulmonary or renal concerns.

questions.question: A student prepares for the (Free Solution) NURS FPX4900 Odom Jonell Assessment2 exam. Which study strategy is most effective?
options: Read only one chapter the night before
questions.explanation: Active practice with rationales helps students retain concepts and improves exam performance better than passive reading.

questions.question: Which medication should the nurse question for a patient with (Free Solution) NURS FPX4900 Odom Jonell Assessment2 and decreased oxygen saturation?
questions.explanation: Sedating opioids can worsen respiratory depression and should be questioned when oxygenation is unstable.

questions.question: What is the most likely priority concern when a patient with (Free Solution) NURS FPX4900 Odom Jonell Assessment2 develops confusion and restlessness?
questions.explanation: Altered mental status in an acute care patient is often an early sign of hypoxia or inadequate perfusion.
